+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-136
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-24_00-25-24+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs — added `EditorKeyCommand::ResetTimeScaleDefault`, Ctrl+0 → Digit0 mapping, and `handle_key_command` arm delegating to `self.set_time_scale(TimeScale::DEFAULT)`.
+- crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs — extended `key_command_mapping_table_is_exact` and `ctrl_shift_combinations_are_unbound` to cover Digit0 / Ctrl+Shift+0 / bare-Digit0 / Shift-only-Digit0.
+- crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs — added `ctrl_0_on_fresh_shell_is_noop_because_default_already_active` and `ctrl_2_then_ctrl_0_after_coalesce_window_resets_and_undo_restores_2x`.
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-136_TASK_2026-05-24_00-13-56+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`:
+  - Added the `ResetTimeScaleDefault` variant to `EditorKeyCommand` (commands.rs:80) with a brief rustdoc that documents the Ctrl+0 binding, the reuse of the existing `set_time_scale` → `SetTimeScale` → `CommandBus::submit` path, and the no-op short-circuit when already at `TimeScale::DEFAULT`.
+  - Added `KeyCode::Digit0 => Self::ResetTimeScaleDefault` to the `from_key_press` match table (commands.rs:117). The pre-existing `if !ctrl || shift { return None; }` guard naturally keeps Ctrl+Shift+0, bare Digit0, and Shift-only Digit0 unbound — no input-surface change required.
+  - Added `EditorKeyCommand::ResetTimeScaleDefault => self.set_time_scale(TimeScale::DEFAULT)` arm to `handle_key_command` (commands.rs:336). Routes through the existing slider mutation path; no direct `TimeScale` / `World` / audit ledger / CommandBus mutation in the handler.
+
+### Tests
+
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs::key_command_mapping_table_is_exact`: added `KeyCode::Digit0 + ctrl=true, shift=false → Some(ResetTimeScaleDefault)` assertion alongside the existing Digit2 row.
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs::ctrl_shift_combinations_are_unbound`: added three Digit0 assertions — Ctrl+Shift+0 → None, bare Digit0 → None, Shift-only Digit0 → None — mirroring the Digit2 guards.
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs::ctrl_0_on_fresh_shell_is_noop_because_default_already_active`: starts a fresh shell at `TimeScale::DEFAULT`, fires `EditorKeyCommand::ResetTimeScaleDefault`, then asserts that the stack length stays at 0, dirty stays false, the slider stays at DEFAULT, and the `TimeScaleChanged` audit count is unchanged.
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs::ctrl_2_then_ctrl_0_after_coalesce_window_resets_and_undo_restores_2x`: fires Ctrl+2 (stack len becomes 1, slider 2.0), sleeps `Duration::from_millis(550)` to exceed the 500 ms `CoalesceWindow::default_500ms` window, then fires Ctrl+0 and asserts (a) slider returns to `TimeScale::DEFAULT`, (b) stack length grows to 2 (distinct entries — no coalesce-merge), (c) a single Ctrl+Z restores the slider to 2.0 (the prior Ctrl+2 value, not DEFAULT).
+
+### Docs
+
+- No doc changes — task forbids docs / ADRs / plans / scripts / schemas / workflows / status files / doctrine files.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` (pre-edit) → empty (no pre-existing tracked changes).
+- `cargo build -p rge-editor-shell` → exit 0.
+- `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` → exit 0. (Initial run failed once on test import-ordering inside `time_scale_test.rs::ctrl_2_then_ctrl_0_..` — fixed by re-ordering `std::*` before `rge_editor_shell::*` per rustfmt's group order; rerun is green.)
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --test keyboard_command_bus_round_trip` → 10 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored (all pre-existing + the extended mapping-table assertions).
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --test time_scale_test` → 19 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored (17 pre-existing + 2 new Ctrl+0 tests).
+- `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` → exit 0 (10 enforcement PASS + 1 supplementary PASS).
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` → exit 0. All 7 verification steps green: fmt, arch lints, arch-lint test suite, cargo deny, workspace tests + doctests, bench --no-run.
+- `git status --short` (post-edit) → exactly the three allowed file modifications plus the pre-existing untracked TASK packet pair and this single EXEC report.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope. The only edited files are the three listed under `MAY edit`; no forbidden file touched; no new production `Action`, no `CompoundAction`, no CommandBus coalesce-policy change. The Ctrl+0 no-op behavior is provided by the existing `set_time_scale` short-circuit; the Ctrl+2-then-Ctrl+0 undo behavior is provided by sleeping past the existing 500 ms `CoalesceWindow` rather than by changing the policy.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- The Ctrl+2-then-Ctrl+0 test uses a 550 ms wall-clock sleep — 50 ms beyond the bus's 500 ms coalesce window. That matches the rapid-drag-coalesce test's stylistic pattern (it relies on calls landing inside the window) and exercises the actual `Instant::now`-based timing path the production code uses. If a deterministic-time helper exists or is desired here (none was found in `rge-editor-actions`), the Planner can route a follow-up; I did not introduce one because the Task Packet forbids new helpers / fixtures.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: 3 modified (the allowed MAY-edit set), no other tracked changes.
+- Untracked items: `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-136_TASK_2026-05-24_00-13-56+0300.md`, `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-136_TASK_2026-05-24_00-13-56+0300.meta.json` (both pre-existing at start of dispatch), plus this `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-136_EXEC_2026-05-24_00-25-24+0300.md` and its `-Finalize` sidecar (allowed by Task Packet).
+- Branch: ai-dispatch/ISSUE-136
+- Last commit: `5aa9013` chore(dispatch): seed task #26 Ctrl+0 CommandBus reset
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-136
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

@@ -0,0 +1,132 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-136
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-24_00-13-56+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs
+- crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs
+- crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-136_EXEC_*.md
+- GitHub issue #136
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Implement GitHub issue #136: add the smallest Ctrl+0 CommandBus time-scale reset in editor-shell. The new binding must map Ctrl+0 to `EditorKeyCommand::ResetTimeScaleDefault`, and handling that command must call `EditorShell::set_time_scale(TimeScale::DEFAULT)` so the existing `SetTimeScale` action and existing `CommandBus::submit` path provide no-op, coalescing, dirty-state, audit, and undo behavior. This dispatch is intentionally narrow: one command enum variant, one key mapping, one handler arm, and focused tests only.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs`
+- `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs`
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `crates/editor-actions/**`
+- `crates/editor-state/**`
+- `editor/rge-editor/**`
+- `kernel/ecs/**`
+- `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`
+- `Cargo.toml`
+- `Cargo.lock`
+- Any `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, workspace manifest, dependency declaration, feature flag, or generated Cargo metadata file.
+- Any editor-shell production source file other than `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`.
+- Any test file other than `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs` and `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs`.
+- Any docs, ADRs, plans, scripts, schemas, workflows, status files, doctrine files, lint configuration, `.gitignore`, generated artifact, persisted log, or repository metadata file.
+- Any existing handoff packet, including this TASK packet, after execution begins.
+- Any GitHub issue, label, PR, branch, comment, review, commit, or remote state.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- Exactly one execution report matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-136_EXEC_*.md`.
+- The matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-136_EXEC_*.meta.json` sidecar only if `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize` creates it while finalizing the EXEC report.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New source files, test files, fixtures, docs, generated artifacts, persisted logs, Cargo entries, dependency files, crates, architecture lints, schemas, scripts, workflow files, TASK packets, REVIEW packets, CORRECT packets, CLOSEOUT packets, issue notes, or design documents.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- `EditorKeyCommand` gains exactly one new production command variant named `ResetTimeScaleDefault`.
+- `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press(KeyCode::Digit0, true, false)` returns `Some(EditorKeyCommand::ResetTimeScaleDefault)`.
+- `EditorShell::handle_key_command(EditorKeyCommand::ResetTimeScaleDefault)` routes by calling `self.set_time_scale(TimeScale::DEFAULT)`.
+- The implementation uses the existing `SetTimeScale` action and the existing `EditorShell::set_time_scale` -> `submit_action` -> `CommandBus::submit` path.
+- Focused tests cover all required behavior:
+  - Digit0 key mapping for Ctrl-without-Shift.
+  - Fresh-shell Ctrl+0 is a no-op because the shell already starts at `TimeScale::DEFAULT`.
+  - Ctrl+2 followed by Ctrl+0 after the CommandBus coalesce window resets `TimeScale` to `TimeScale::DEFAULT`.
+  - Undo after that Ctrl+0 reset restores the prior 2.0 time scale.
+- One `EXECUTION_REPORT` packet documents the exact files changed, verification commands run, results, and any deviations or halt condition.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The only production behavior change is Ctrl+0 reset-to-default time scale through `EditorKeyCommand::ResetTimeScaleDefault`.
+- No new `Action` trait shape, no new production `Action` implementation, no adapter ledger, no adapter authority layer, no `CompoundAction` wrapper, and no CommandBus coalesce-window change is introduced.
+- `EditorShell::handle_key_command` does not directly mutate `TimeScale`, `World`, resources, audit ledgers, or CommandBus internals for Ctrl+0; it delegates to `set_time_scale(TimeScale::DEFAULT)`.
+- The existing no-op behavior in `EditorShell::set_time_scale` remains authoritative for fresh-shell Ctrl+0. A Ctrl+0 while already at `TimeScale::DEFAULT` must not submit an action, grow the undo stack, flip the dirty flag, or record a `TimeScaleChanged` audit event.
+- Existing Ctrl+Z, Ctrl+Y, Ctrl+S, and Ctrl+2 mappings and behavior remain unchanged.
+- Ctrl+Shift+0 is unbound unless the current mapping signature makes that impossible without broader input-surface changes; in that case halt with `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+- The Ctrl+2 then Ctrl+0 reset test must wait long enough to exceed the existing CommandBus coalesce window before sending Ctrl+0, so the reset is a distinct undoable entry and undo returns to 2.0.
+- The tests are placed only in `crates/editor-shell/tests/keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs` and/or `crates/editor-shell/tests/time_scale_test.rs`.
+- The implementation compiles without touching any forbidden file or adding Cargo dependencies.
+- The final diff contains no preset trio, step-up/step-down commands, UI, menu, toolbar, egui, editor binary, Cargo, editor-actions, editor-state, ECS, or architecture-doctrine changes.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not add Ctrl+1, Ctrl+3, a preset trio, step-up/step-down commands, playback commands, UI, menu items, toolbar buttons, status-bar changes, egui wiring, editor binary event rewrites, or any visible control beyond the existing keyboard command dispatch path.
+- Do not change the `Action` trait, `CommandBus`, `UndoStack`, action payload semantics, coalescing policy, audit-ledger design, editor-state APIs, ECS resource APIs, or architecture-lint policy.
+- Do not add a new production action for reset-to-default. Ctrl+0 must reuse the existing `SetTimeScale` action via `EditorShell::set_time_scale(TimeScale::DEFAULT)`.
+- Do not move command code out of `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`.
+- Do not edit `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`; the existing keyboard dispatch into `EditorKeyCommand::from_key_press` must remain sufficient.
+- Do not broaden tests into GPU, render-path, egui, editor binary, or workspace-wide behavior beyond the required gates.
+- Do not commit, push, open a PR, create or edit GitHub issues, or write follow-up packets.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these commands in the `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` before editing. Document any pre-existing tracked changes. Do not clean, revert, or modify unrelated user changes.
+- `cargo build -p rge-editor-shell` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo +nightly fmt --all -- --check` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --test keyboard_command_bus_round_trip` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo test -p rge-editor-shell --test time_scale_test` -> expected exit 0.
+- `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` -> expected exit 0.
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` -> expected exit 0.
+- `git status --short` after all edits and gates. Expected Executor-created changes are limited to the three allowed edit files plus the single `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-136_EXEC_*.md` report and optional matching `.meta.json` sidecar.
+
+If a required verification command fails because of behavior inside the allowed files, fix within scope and rerun the failed gate plus any affected focused tests. If a required verification command fails because of unrelated pre-existing work outside the allowed scope, do not fix outside scope; document the failure and halt with the appropriate status described below.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt (without committing) and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- `rge_input::KeyCode::Digit0` is unavailable or cannot be referenced from `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs` without editing forbidden files.
+- An existing Ctrl+0 binding is present in the current editor-shell command surface and adding this binding would shadow or change it.
+- The command surface has moved enough that implementing Ctrl+0 requires editing `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs`, editor binary event code, egui/menu/toolbar code, editor-state, editor-actions, ECS, Cargo files, or any other forbidden surface.
+- Routing Ctrl+0 through `EditorShell::set_time_scale(TimeScale::DEFAULT)` would not use the existing `SetTimeScale` action and existing `CommandBus::submit` path.
+- Passing the required tests requires adding a new production `Action` implementation, changing the `Action` trait, creating an adapter ledger, wrapping in `CompoundAction`, or changing CommandBus internals.
+- Passing the Ctrl+2 then Ctrl+0 undo requirement requires changing the existing CommandBus coalesce-window duration or coalescing policy.
+- A required test cannot be written in `keyboard_command_bus_round_trip.rs` or `time_scale_test.rs` without new helper crates, new fixtures, GPU/render setup, or forbidden file edits.
+- The fresh-shell Ctrl+0 no-op behavior cannot be preserved with the existing `set_time_scale` no-op short-circuit.
+- Any required verification failure points to code outside the allowed edit set and fixing it would require touching a forbidden file.
+- Any required file is missing, unreadable, or already changed in a way that makes the bounded implementation unsafe to perform without human review.
+- The Executor accidentally edits a forbidden file or creates a forbidden artifact. Stop immediately, do not continue expanding the diff, and report the deviation.
+
+If a halt condition fires before code changes are safe, write the single EXEC report if possible with header `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, include `EXEC_STATUS: blocked`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`, and `EXIT_CODE: 0`. If a narrow missing-context blocker is resolvable by the Planner without human architecture judgment, use `STATUS: BLOCKED`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: BLOCKED`, and `NEXT_ROLE: PLANNER_AI` instead.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+The relevant current shape is already in `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/commands.rs`: `SetTimeScale` exists, `EditorShell::set_time_scale` captures `from`, clamps `to`, short-circuits no-ops, submits through `CommandBus`, and records the editor-shell time-scale audit event only after a successful non-no-op submit. Ctrl+2 already uses this path through `EditorKeyCommand::SetTimeScaleDoubleSpeed`; Ctrl+0 should add the reset command entry point to that same path, not invent a second time-scale mutation surface.
+
+The reset-after-Ctrl+2 test must account for existing coalescing. If Ctrl+0 is submitted inside the 500 ms coalesce window, it can merge into the prior `SetTimeScale` stack entry and undo will not return to 2.0. The required behavior for this dispatch is specifically Ctrl+2, then wait past the existing coalesce window, then Ctrl+0, then undo back to 2.0. Do not change the coalesce policy to satisfy the test.
+
+Under the orchestrator protocol, no `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packet is expected from this TASK. The only new packet produced by execution should be the `ISSUE-136_EXEC_*.md` report, plus its optional generated sidecar.
